Using Claude Code and context engineering to automate a marketing outreach workflow for a charisma coach, specifically building a system to research, evaluate, and generate presence reports for 150 plastic surgeons as potential coaching clients.

context engineering
AI automation
lead generation
outreach automation

marketing workflow
presence report
charisma coaching
Expert coaches and consultants who want to automate client prospecting and outreach at scale (100+ leads)
Public speaking coaches, charisma coaches, and performance coaches targeting professional or high-income niches
Professionals with intermediate-to-advanced AI tool familiarity — comfortable with Claude Code, ChatGPT prompt engineering, and CSV data workflows, but not necessarily software developers
Business owners and solo practitioners doing manual lead research who want to transition to semi-automated marketing pipelines
People already experimenting with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) for business development but hitting scaling bottlenecks — the transcript assumes prior exposure to prompt engineering concepts
YouTube creators in the AI/automation education space — the 'Happy Dave / AppyDave' brand targets viewers who learn by watching real workflow builds rather than polished tutorials
Viewers interested in Context Engineering as a practical business skill, not just a theoretical concept — the content positions CE as accessible to non-engineers

Secondary audience: the client persona (Guy Munro) represents coaches in any niche who serve affluent professional markets (surgeons, lawyers, executives) and need credible, research-backed outreach material
aspiration and ambition — the promise of turning expert knowledge into scalable outreach inspires viewers who want to grow their business
social proof and credibility — featuring a real client (Guy) and real use case validates the approach and reduces skepticism
curiosity and discovery — iterative live problem-solving keeps viewers watching to see what happens next
empowerment — showing non-technical experts how to automate complex workflows makes the viewer feel capable
excitement of automation — the idea of generating 150 hot leads in days triggers excitement about leverage and efficiency
relatability — acknowledging confusion, mistakes, and course-corrections makes the process feel accessible and honest
urgency and opportunity — framing plastic surgeons as an underserved market needing help creates a sense of untapped potential
mastery and expertise — the coach superpower framing appeals to viewers who see themselves as experts wanting broader impact
trust through transparency — showing real tools, real files, and real limitations builds authenticity
anticipation — ending with a teaser for the next video creates a cliffhanger that drives return views

150 target individuals (plastic surgeons) in the outreach list; 150 CSV entries; 4 test plastic surgeons found via web search (2 from Sydney Australia: Jeremy Hunt and Ellis Choi; 2 from London: Jan Steneck and Sijatha Tadapathy); 36% context remaining in Claude at one point during the session; goal of potentially having 150 hot leads within a couple of days; 9 content pillars mentioned in ecosystem docs (not transcript-specific).

I love this iteration process where you do something with a good prompt, but then you use feedback from the system to improve the prompt

I'm Appy Dave, see you in the next video.

Context engineering with Claude Code can automate repetitive research and outreach workflows — in this case, profiling 150 plastic surgeons for a coaching business — reducing days of manual work to a structured, repeatable pipeline.

Start with the human expert's existing work (spreadsheets, ChatGPT prompts, scorecards) and use it as the seed for automation rather than building from scratch — this preserves domain knowledge while scaling it.
Iterative prompt improvement is built into the workflow: after each research output, gaps are identified and fed back to refine the prompts, so quality compounds across all 150 runs.
Separating raw source data from working outputs (a raw folder vs. a dots/working folder) and enforcing consistent naming conventions keeps the automated pipeline organised and debuggable.
Using test data (publicly available surgeons from other countries) before running on real targets lets you validate and fix the workflow without exposing real leads to a broken process.

Avoid rigid templates for AI-generated reports — guidelines (like formatting hints) give the model freedom to surface the best available data for each unique subject, rather than forcing uniform output.

A human expert still owns the scorecard and assessment step — automation handles research and report generation, but the coach's judgment on charisma and public speaking gaps remains the irreplaceable differentiator.

The end goal is a personalised HTML or PDF presence report per prospect that doubles as a value-first outreach tool — giving the coach a foot in the door by demonstrating expertise before any sales conversation.
casual tutorial
Are you an expert who can watch someone for 15 minutes and know exactly what to tweak or advice to give to take them to the next level?
How could you connect with 150 potentially target individuals and create an outreach report or connection that gets you a foot in the door?
How can you automate the marketing workflow using context engineering?
Can the system automatically delete old files and clean up after itself?
Where is the master prompt and is it any good?
Could gaps in the data have been filled by improving the prompts?
Do we want a fact sheet and presence report as two separate files, or combined into one?
Should the workflow document be updated to reflect the new structure?
Should the system use a rigid template or more flexible formatting guidelines?
Is it better to perfect the starter prompts before moving forward in the workflow, or proceed to the next stage?
What is the YouTube presence, LinkedIn presence, or other media information available for each surgeon?
When there is no video content available for a surgeon, should the system skip, request a consultation recording, or mark as insufficient digital presence?
Should gap-filling prompt suggestions be approved or rejected?
Could simple Q&A questions be prompted for the coach to look into during the outreach process?

automate lead generation with Claude AI
context engineering marketing workflow Claude

automated client research with Claude Code
how to use Claude Code for business outreach
AI lead generation spreadsheet automation
context engineering 150 leads automation
Claude Code web search research reports
Real client collaboration — the workflow is built live alongside an actual paying client (Guy Munro, a charisma coach), making it a genuine use case rather than a fabricated demo

Niche-specific outreach automation — targets a very specific professional segment (plastic surgeons as public speaking coaching prospects), showing how to build a hyper-targeted lead generation pipeline rather than generic CRM automation

Context Engineering as the core methodology — explicitly frames the automation approach as Context Engineering rather than generic prompt engineering, distinguishing the technique and giving it a named identity

Iterative prompt self-improvement loop — demonstrates Claude rewriting its own research prompts based on gap analysis feedback, showing a meta-learning workflow where the system improves itself as it processes each record

Human-in-the-loop scorecard design — explicitly preserves the expert evaluation step (Guy watching videos to score charisma) rather than trying to fully automate judgment, showing a realistic hybrid human-AI workflow

Scale framing from day one — the entire system is designed with 150 leads in mind, so every architectural decision (folder naming conventions, indexed file structure, combined vs split reports) is justified by the need to repeat the process at scale

Live CLAUDE.md management — shows treating the Claude system prompt as a living document that stays in sync with the evolving project structure, a workflow discipline rarely demonstrated in tutorial content

Test-first approach with synthetic data — uses publicly available plastic surgeons from other countries as safe test data before running on the real 150-person target list, showing professional QA discipline applied to AI workflows

Gap-driven prompt refinement — introduces a structured gap analysis pattern where missing data triggers prompt improvement suggestions, turning failures into systematic enhancements rather than one-off fixes

Side-by-side report design intent — plans to place the coach's video commentary next to the surgeon's online presence in the final HTML report, creating a differentiated outreach artifact that functions as a personalised sales asset rather than a generic cold email

value.prompt: Typography-dominant YouTube thumbnail on near-black #25201e background. Left-aligned Bebas Neue hook text 'PROSPECT ON AUTOPILOT' in bright yellow #ffde59, uppercase, large, occupying top 40% of frame. Thin horizontal gold #ccba9d rule below hook text spanning 55% width. Below rule, cream #faf5ec Bebas Neue secondary line 'CLAUDE CODE FINDS YOUR CLIENTS'. Right edge bleeds a ghost watermark of the word 'AUTOMATE' in bold uppercase Oswald, dark brown on near-black at 10% opacity, slightly rotated, clipped by frame edge. No humans, no 3D, no neon, no halftone. AppyDave palette only.
